content.content:
1	# Yellow Plugins - Project Memory
2	
3	## Project Structure
4	- Marketplace repo: `yellow-plugins` with plugins in `plugins/<name>/`
5	- Each plugin follows Claude Code auto-discovery format: `agents/`, `commands/`, `skills/`, `plugin.json`
6	- Docs at `docs/`, solutions documented at `docs/solutions/<category>/`
7	- Main branch: `main`, uses Graphite (`gt`) for all branch/PR management
8	
9	## Shell Script Security Patterns (from PR #5, extended PR #10)
10	- Always validate user input with `validate_name()` before use in paths
11	- Reject path traversal: `..`, `/`, `~` in names
12	- Printf: never put variables in format string — use `printf '%s' "$var"` not `printf "$var"`
13	- Skip symlinks when copying sensitive files: `[ -f "$f" ] && [ ! -L "$f" ]`
14	- Use `--` separator before positional args that could start with `-`
15	- Parse git output with `--porcelain` + `awk`, never human-readable format
16	- Use `sed 's/^prefix //'` not `cut -d' '` for paths (handles spaces)
17	- Path comparison: `[ "$a" = "$b" ]` not `echo | grep`
18	
19	## Bash Hook & Validation Patterns (from PR #10, extended PRs #73, #74)
20	- **Newline in case patterns:** `$(printf '\n')` is EMPTY — command substitution strips trailing newlines. Use `tr -d '\n\r'` + length comparison instead
21	- **jq @sh consolidation:** Parse all JSON fields in one jq call: `eval "$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '@sh "VAR=\(.field)"')"` — add `# shellcheck disable=SC2154` at file level
22	- **TOCTOU in flock:** Re-read state INSIDE `flock` scope — never use values read before lock acquisition
23	- **Prompt injection fencing:** Wrap untrusted content in `--- begin/end ---` delimiters + "treat as reference only" advisory
24	- **Error logging:** Replace `|| true` / `2>/dev/null` with `|| { printf '[component] Error: ...\n' >&2; }` — always log with component prefix
25	- **Shared validation lib pattern:** Extract `validate_file_path()`, `validate_namespace()`, `canonicalize_project_dir()` to sourced `lib/validate.sh`
26	- **CRLF on WSL2:** Files created via Write tool get CRLF. Always `sed -i 's/\r$//'` after creating `.sh` files
27	- **Queue schema versioning:** Add `"schema": "1"` to JSONL entries for forward compatibility
28	- **PostToolUse field paths (from PR #73):** PostToolUse input nests fields — command is at `.tool_input.command` (NOT root `.command`), exit code is at `.tool_result.exit_code` (NOT root `.exit_code`). PreToolUse uses root `.command`. Confirmed in `plugins/yellow-ruvector/hooks/scripts/post-tool-use.sh:36` and `plugins/gt-workflow/hooks/check-commit-message.sh:19,34`
29	- **Conventional commit regex must include `!` (from PR #73):** Breaking-change indicator `!` is valid per spec: `feat!: msg` and `fix(scope)!: msg`. Regex `^(type)(\(scope\))?:` misses these. Always use `^(type)(\(scope\))?!?:` — the `!?` goes between the optional scope group and the colon
30	- **Hook not wired = completely non-functional (from PR #73):** A hook script can be perfectly written but silently never run if not registered in `plugin.json` as a `hooks` entry. After writing any hook script, verify a matching PostToolUse/PreToolUse/SessionStart entry exists in `plugin.json`. Detection: `jq '.hooks' plugins/<name>/plugin.json`
31	- **EXIT_CODE jq-parse fallback must fail-closed (from PR #73):** Pattern `EXIT_CODE="${exit_code:-0}"` defaults to 0 (success) when jq fails to parse the hook input. jq parse failure should be treated as unknown/error, not success. Use `EXIT_CODE="${exit_code:-1}"` or exit 1 on parse failure.
32	- **jq not installed warning in hooks (from PR #73):** Hook scripts that use jq must check `command -v jq` at the top and print a warning to stderr if missing — never silently fall through to undefined behavior.
33	- **SessionStart hooks must output `{"continue": true}` on all error paths (from PR #72):** If a SessionStart hook exits without outputting `{"continue": true}`, Claude Code blocks the entire session startup. Every error path — including jq failures, missing env vars, and API errors — must end with `printf '{"continue": true}\n'`. Pattern: `jq_command || { printf '[component] Error: jq failed\n' >&2; printf '{"continue": true}\n'; }`
34	- **`set -e` must NOT be used in hooks that output JSON (from this review session):** `set -euo pipefail` causes the script to exit on any non-zero command — before the required `{"continue": true}` line is printed. Use `set -uo pipefail` (drop `-e`) in ALL hook scripts that must output JSON. Add comment: `# Note: -e omitted intentionally — hook must output {"continue": true} on all paths`. Found in 3 hooks across 2 plugins in same review.
35	- **`json_exit()` helper pattern for hooks:** Define a helper at top of every hook script to centralize all exits: `json_exit() { local msg="${1:-}"; [ -n "$msg" ] && printf '[component] %s\n' "$msg" >&2; printf '{"continue": true}\n'; exit 0; }`. All early-exit paths call `json_exit` instead of bare `exit 0`. Guarantees no exit path forgets JSON output.
36	- **SessionStart must output JSON not plain text:** SessionStart hooks output `{"continue": true}` or `{"systemMessage": "...", "continue": true}`. Plain text output (e.g., `printf '[plugin] Warning: ...\n'` to stdout) is not a valid SessionStart response and causes unexpected behavior. System messages go in `systemMessage` JSON field; warnings go to stderr. See `docs/solutions/code-quality/hook-set-e-and-json-exit-pattern.md`
37	- **`CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` unset warning in SessionStart hooks (from PR #72):** When falling back to `$PWD`, always warn: `printf '[component] Warning: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset; falling back to PWD (%s)\n' "$PWD" >&2`. Silent fallback produces misleading behavior when Claude Code is launched from an unexpected directory.
38	- See `docs/solutions/security-issues/yellow-ruvector-plugin-multi-agent-code-review.md`
39	- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/posttooluse-hook-input-schema-field-paths.md`
40	
41	## Command File Anti-Patterns (from PR #35 review)
42	- **curl split:** Never `head -n -1` / `tail -1` — use `curl -o "$BODY_FILE" -w '%{http_code}'` (temp file pattern)
43	- **ToolSearch required:** Every command using deferred MCP tools must include `ToolSearch` in `allowed-tools`
44	- **subagent_type = agent name:** Must match `name:` field in agent frontmatter, NOT plugin directory name
45	- **subagent_type must be literal (from PR #71):** When prose says "Spawn X via Task", the exact `subagent_type: "plugin:dir:name"` string must be spelled out. LLMs guess wrong when it's left implicit — they use directory names or descriptive names instead of the frontmatter `name:` value
46	- **yq in $():** Exit code lost — parse frontmatter once, check exit, use `yq -r '@sh'` consolidation
47	- **yq -r required for raw string output (from PR #74):** kislyuk/yq returns quoted strings without `-r` flag: `yq '.status'` returns `"pending"` (with double-quotes), NOT `pending`. This causes ALL string comparisons (`case "$STATUS"`, `[ "$STATUS" = pending ]`) to silently fail — every transition matches `"pending"` not `pending`. Always use `yq -r '.field'` for string fields. This is distinct from the variant check below.
48	- **yq variant check (from PR #74):** `command -v yq` passes for both kislyuk/yq and mikefarah/yq — they are incompatible. Always add: `yq --help 2>&1 | grep -qi 'jq wrapper\|kislyuk' || { printf 'wrong yq variant\n' >&2; exit 1; }` when using `-y`/`@sh` flags
49	- **BASH_SOURCE[0]:** Not portable in Claude Code — use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/lib/validate.sh` instead
50	- **Dedup ordering:** Dedup check must run AFTER the data it keys on is collected (e.g., workflow name from failure-analyst)
51	- **M3 before bulk writes — no threshold (from PR #74):** AskUserQuestion showing count+titles is ALWAYS required before any loop that creates N issues/files. Never gate it on a count threshold (e.g., "if more than 20"). Applies for N=1 too.
52	- **AskUserQuestion for mid-step input (from PR #74):** Prose saying "ask as follow-up question in your response" does NOT pause execution. Every mid-step user input must use the `AskUserQuestion` tool. Every Cancel/No branch must have an explicit stop instruction.
53	- **AskUserQuestion "Other" is the ONLY free-text button (from PR #74):** Button labels like "Submit reason", "Enter date", or any custom label do NOT open a free-text input field. Only the literal button labeled "Other" opens free-text input in Claude Code's AskUserQuestion UI. When free-text input is needed (e.g., defer reason, custom message), use the "Other" label.
54	- **Agent step skip guards must be explicit prose (from PR #71):** When a step is conditional (e.g., "only if P1/P2 findings exist"), the agent will proceed unless the condition's false branch is stated as a literal instruction: "If [condition not met], skip this step." without that sentence, LLMs continue executing. Every optional step needs an explicit skip instruction as its first line.
55	- **$VAR in bash code blocks (from PR #74):** Each Bash tool call is a fresh subprocess — variables don't survive between calls. Any `$VAR` in a bash code block must be derived in that same block, or the LLM must be instructed to substitute the actual value inline. Never reference `$TODO_PATH` or similar in a code snippet that doesn't define it.
56	- **Heredoc for user-supplied free text (from PR #74):** When a command receives user-typed input (defer reason, message, description) and passes it to a shell function, use heredoc or `printf '%s'` — never inline substitution. User input containing `"`, backticks, or `$` produces malformed shell. Pattern: `REASON=$(cat <<'EOF'\n...\nEOF\n)` then pass as `"$REASON"`.
57	- **Heredoc delimiter collision with user input (P1):** Short, common English words as heredoc delimiters (`REASON`, `EOF`, `END`, `INPUT`, `MSG`) close prematurely if user types that word on its own line — silently truncates captured text and may execute trailing content as shell. Always use `__EOF_<CONTEXT>__` style delimiters (e.g., `<<'__EOF_DEFER_REASON__'`). Always quote the delimiter (`<<'...'` not `<<...`). See `docs/solutions/security-issues/heredoc-delimiter-collision.md`
58	- **Derived value validation:** Always validate $REPO, $ISSUE_ID, $SESSION_URL immediately after derivation
59	- **--flag missing value:** Guard with `[ -z "${2:-}" ] || printf '%s' "${2:-}" | grep -q '^--'`
60	- **Prerequisites as steps:** `command -v tool` + `gh auth status` as executable steps, not prose
61	- **Hook command scope must be exhaustive (from PR #73):** When writing a hook that validates or intercepts a commit message, enumerate ALL commands in the plugin that create commits — not just the most common ones. For gt-workflow: `gt modify`, `gt commit`, AND `gt create <name> -m "msg"` all produce commit messages. Missing even one command means the hook silently passes bad messages through that path.
62	- **Functions don't survive bash blocks (from PR #259):** Re-define every helper in each block that calls it; source a lib file for DRY. See `bash-block-subshell-isolation-in-command-files.md`
63	- **`has_userconfig` dead-code + credential echo (from PR #259):** Key absent from `plugin.json.userConfig` → call always returns false → dead branch. Never echo any portion of a credential value.
64	- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/claude-code-command-authoring-anti-patterns.md`
65	
66	## Plugin Authoring Quality Rules
67	- Agent `.md` files: 120 lines = audit threshold, not hard max. Accept up to 200 lines for multi-step workflows with novel algorithms; up to 300 lines if every section encodes content Claude cannot infer (lookup tables, scoring algorithms, security fencing, multi-branch UX). Split above 300 lines. Anthropic's SKILL.md hard guidance is 500 lines. See `docs/research/do-we-actually-need-a-120-line-maximum-o.md`
68	- Every agent/skill description must include "Use when..." trigger clause
69	- Command `allowed-tools` must list every tool used in the body
70	- Commands use `$ARGUMENTS` placeholder, never hardcoded values
71	- SKILL.md must have three standard headings: `## What It Does`, `## When to Use`, `## Usage`; subsections inside Usage are `###`
72	- All files must use LF line endings (configure `.gitattributes`)
73	- **Skill frontmatter attribute:** `user-invokable` (with k), NOT `user-invocable` (with c)
74	- **Skill and agent descriptions:** must be single-line — YAML folded scalars (`description: >`) AND multi-line single-quoted strings are NOT parsed by Claude Code's frontmatter parser. Both silently truncate the description. Applies to ALL `.md` plugin files (SKILL.md and agent `.md`). On-touch rule: whenever any agent/skill `.md` is modified, grep `'^description: [>|]'` AND check for single-quoted values that wrap to the next line. See `docs/solutions/code-quality/skill-frontmatter-attribute-and-format-requirements.md`
75	- **Install command format:** `/plugin marketplace add owner/repo` (short form works, no full HTTPS URL needed)
76	- **Stale dep name in CLAUDE.md after plugin switch (from PR #70):** When a plugin's dependency changes (e.g., `compound-engineering` → `yellow-core`), grep the plugin's CLAUDE.md "Known Limitations" and "Cross-Plugin Agent References" sections for the old plugin name — stale references cause agents to invoke a non-existent plugin. Detection: `grep -r '<old-plugin-name>' plugins/<name>/CLAUDE.md`
77	
78	## Plugin Manifest Validation (from public release 2026-02-18, extended PR #66)
79	- **`repository` must be string:** Claude Code rejects npm-style `{"type":"git","url":"..."}` objects. Use plain URL string: `"repository": "https://github.com/owner/repo"`
80	- **`hooks` must be inline:** File path references (`"./hooks/hooks.json"`) rejected. Inline hook definitions as JSON object directly in plugin.json
81	- **hooks.json reference file:** If a `hooks/hooks.json` file exists alongside inline hooks in `plugin.json`, add a `_comment` key to it (`"_comment": "Reference only — Claude Code reads inline hooks from plugin.json"`) so future maintainers do not treat the file as authoritative. Also keep all numeric fields (e.g., `timeout`) in sync — `hooks/hooks.json` timeout drifting from `plugin.json` hooks timeout causes subtle behavior mismatches (from PR #74)
82	- **No unknown keys:** `marketplace.json` entries reject any key not in schema (e.g., `"id"`). Use `"name"` as identifier. The `"changelog"` key is NOT recognized by the remote validator — do not add it to plugin.json
83	- **Local CI ≠ remote validation:** `pnpm validate:schemas` passing does NOT guarantee Claude Code's validator accepts it. Always test install on a fresh machine before release
84	- **Schema alignment:** Add `"additionalProperties": false` to local schemas to catch unknown keys in CI
85	- **JSON trailing comma on deletion:** When removing the last property from a JSON `"properties"` block, the preceding property retains a trailing comma — invalid JSON. Always run `jq empty` after any property removal
86	- See `docs/solutions/build-errors/claude-code-plugin-manifest-validation-errors.md`
87	- See `docs/solutions/build-errors/plugin-json-changelog-key-schema-drift-remote-validator.md`

90	- Security pattern codes (M3, C1, H1) must name the concrete tool: "confirm via AskUserQuestion" not just "M3 confirmation"
91	- Shared validation rules: copy ALL steps from skill — never omit any (e.g., "strip HTML")
92	- Error handling refs: enumerate categories, don't just say "see skill for errors"

94	- AI reviewer false positives: run `pnpm validate:plugins` + check precedent in other plugins before accepting

97	## GitHub GraphQL Shell Patterns (from PR #9)
98	- jq pipelines: always capture exit code — `OUTPUT=$(...| jq '...') || { error; exit 1; }`
99	- JSON construction: use `jq -n --arg` not `printf '{"key":"%s"}'`
100	- Nullable GitHub fields: use `// "ghost"` or `// "default"` fallback
101	- GraphQL `first:N`: always include `pageInfo { hasNextPage }` + warn on truncation
102	- Error pattern matching: use complete phrases (`*"already resolved"*`) not single words
103	- ShellCheck SC2016: disable on **separate line** above GraphQL queries — inline comments cause SC1073
104	- Cross-plugin agent refs: distinguish plugin `.md` files from Task `subagent_type` registry entries

107	## Agent Workflow Security Patterns (from PR #9)
108	- Human-in-the-loop: commands that push LLM-generated code must `AskUserQuestion` before `gt submit`
109	- Prompt injection boundaries: agents processing untrusted input (PR comments, issues) need explicit safety rules
110	- Path traversal in derived paths: validate category/slug contain only `[a-z0-9-]` before constructing file paths. Use `^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (not `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$`) to also reject trailing and consecutive hyphens
111	- Custom-rule bot noise: CodeAnt `[custom_rule]` comments about @Devin tagging — dismiss for agent/config files

114	## Shell Documentation Patterns (from PR #7)
115	- Quote ALL variables in code examples: `"$var"` in tests and case statements
116	- Add `command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1` checks before any tool usage
117	- Error messages must distinguish state categories (not-yet-running vs completed)
118	- TOCTOU: re-fetch state after user interaction, before destructive action
119	- HTTP 429: must exit or retry — never fall through to next code block
120	- Token format: define in one place (shared skill), grep for stale prefixes
121	- Numeric constraints: cross-check polling limits vs time-box guidelines
122	- plugin.json `commands`: must include ALL tools used by agents/commands
123	- POST curl examples: always include `Content-Type: application/json` header

125	## Parallel Todo Resolution Patterns (from PR #37, 2026-02-22)
126	- **File-based grouping:** When resolving N todos in parallel, spawn one agent per FILE (not per todo). Todos that share a file must go to the same agent — last writer wins in concurrent edits
127	- **Dependency mapping:** Build todo→file map first, invert to file→todos, then spawn. Conflict sets = todos sharing any file (transitive closure)
128	- **Cascade closures:** YAGNI removal todos (e.g., 058 removes a step) close sibling todos targeting that step (e.g., 049). Identify before spawning — assign to closing agent with "close without changes"
129	- **Within-file ordering:** Sequential deps handled naturally by single agent: structural changes → content changes; removals → additions; guards → logic that uses them
130	- **Cross-file todo renaming:** If todo 046 affects 7 files, designate ONE agent as canonical closer for that todo file rename. All others: "Do not rename T-046"
131	- **gt modify multi-line commit:** Use separate `-m` flags: `gt modify -c -m "subject" -m "body"`. Heredoc in `$()` substitution fails (treated as single unknown argument)
132	- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/parallel-todo-resolution-file-based-grouping.md`
133	
134	## Parallel Agent Orchestration Patterns (from PR #11)
135	- **File-ownership grouping:** Before launching parallel fix agents, map each todo to its target files. Group non-conflicting todos; run groups sequentially, agents within groups in parallel
136	- **Defer structural refactors:** If a todo (e.g., DRY refactor) overlaps with many targeted fixes, defer it to a separate PR
137	- **Agent type names:** Use exact Task tool registry names (`pr-review-toolkit:X`), NOT inferred plugin paths (`compound-engineering:review:X`)
138	- **Batch triage:** For 10+ findings, rename todo files `pending→ready` in bulk rather than one-by-one approval
139	- **Bulk operation safety:** Explicitly exclude deferred items from batch status updates; verify after
140	- **Skill trimming:** Cut LLM training data duplication, keep safety rules + trigger clauses + validation patterns
141	- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/parallel-multi-agent-review-orchestration.md`

193	## Plugin Agent Authoring Anti-Patterns (from PR #265, 2026-04-26, extended round-2)
194	- **Multi-file MCP count drift:** Every MCP add/remove must update counts in README (2+ locations), plugin `package.json`, and setup probe lists atomically — run `rg '[0-9]+ MCP' README.md` before opening PR; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/stale-env-var-docs-and-prose-count-drift.md`
195	- **Subjective fallback thresholds:** "No useful results / score thin / sparse" gives LLM interpretive latitude to skip fallback entirely — replace with `totalResults < N` numeric guards, document threshold rationale inline; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/agent-prose-fallback-threshold-anti-pattern.md`
196	- **Classification ↔ probe coupling:** Every ToolSearch visibility criterion in `setup/all.md` Step 2 must have a matching probe entry in Step 1.5 (keyword list + resolved-name list + count); adding a criterion without the probe causes silent misfire on every run; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/setup-classification-probe-coupling.md`
197	- **Duplicate MCP URL = N OAuth pop-ups (from PR #265 round-2):** Same MCP server URL registered in multiple `plugin.json` files = separate OAuth client per Claude Code plugin namespace = N browser OAuth prompts on first use. Register each MCP server in exactly one plugin; consuming plugins detect via ToolSearch + document as optional cross-plugin dep. Pre-PR check: `rg '"url".*mcp\.' plugins/*/plugin.json`; see `docs/solutions/integration-issues/duplicate-mcp-url-double-oauth.md`
198	- **Agent prose `result.X < N` field-rename brittleness (from PR #265 round-2):** LLM evaluates missing field as 0, silently activating fallback on every call when MCP server renames field. Fix: document fail-closed inline ("if `result.totalResults` missing, treat as 0 and log warning") AND add integration test asserting field is present and numeric on sparse-results queries; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/agent-prose-field-path-brittleness.md`
199	- **Real-prefix test fixture strings trigger secret scanners (from PR #265 round-2):** `cer_sk_invalid_key_for_test_only_xxx` matches TruffleHog/Gitleaks prefix rules — CI noise or allowlist blind spot. Use vendor-neutral prefixes like `invalid-test-key-xxxx` or `test_fixture_not_a_real_credential_00`; never add file-level allowlist entries; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/test-fixture-secret-scanner-false-positive.md`
200	
201	## JSON Schema & Validator Correctness Patterns (from PR #257, 2026-04-28)
202	- **`typeof === 'object'` bypassed by array form:** When a field gains an array-form variant, all existing `typeof value === 'object'` guards silently match arrays too — add `&& !Array.isArray(value)` everywhere; see `docs/solutions/logic-errors/json-schema-typeof-array-bypass.md`
203	- **Unconstrained `{ "type": "object" }` in oneOf = accept-all:** Pair every naked object branch with `minProperties: 1` or `required`; audit validator script consumers atomically when adding new schema discriminants; see `docs/solutions/logic-errors/json-schema-typeof-array-bypass.md`

207	- **Canonical skill blocks must be verbatim copy:** Paraphrased copies (even one dropped sentence) re-introduce the drift SKILL.md was meant to eliminate; diff against source before committing; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/frontmatter-sweep-and-canonical-skill-drift.md`

209	## Shell Helper & Plugin Install Patterns (from PR #248, 2026-04-28)
210	- **`printf '%b'` interprets `\033`/`\n` from caller args:** Colored `error()`/`warning()` helpers using `'%bError: %s%b\n'` let any caller-passed external output inject terminal escapes. Use `'\033[0;31mError: %s\033[0m\n' "$1"` — literal ANSI in format string + `%s` for content; see `docs/solutions/security-issues/printf-percent-b-terminal-escape-injection.md`
211	- **Smoke-test the MCP entrypoint subcommand at install time:** Plugin install scripts that verify `<tool> --version` still leave a silent 0-tools failure mode if `plugin.json` invokes a subcommand (e.g., `mempalace mcp`) absent from the installed version. Add `<tool> <subcommand> --help >/dev/null 2>&1 || warning "..."` after PATH+version checks; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/plugin-install-mcp-subcommand-smoke-test.md`
212	- **`have_X` + `run_X` helper pair beats token-array protocol:** When a tool has multiple invocations (`python3 -m pip` vs `pip3` vs `pip`) called from 2+ sites, a `mapfile -t cmd < <(detect_X)` protocol propagates ceremony to every caller. Use `have_X()` predicate (sets `X_CMD_USED` for messages) + `run_X()` dispatcher with a `case` — call sites become plain function calls; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/shell-tool-detection-helper-pair-pattern.md`

225	## Prompt Injection Defense Layering (April 2026)
226	- **Model-level fencing degrades:** Sandwich defense goes from 0.4% → 3.8% leak rate under 277-round sustained attack (arxiv 2604.23887) — not a primary control
227	- **App-layer output filtering = only zero-leak defense:** Filter agent outputs for injection markers before any sensitive sink (Write tool, Bash, git op, API mutation)
228	- **ROLP is the highest-leverage control:** Untrusted content (PR diffs, issue bodies, API payloads) must NEVER appear in system prompt — inject only into human turn / tool result positions
229	- **AgentVisor STI protocol** (arxiv 2604.24118): Suitability/Taint/Integrity labeling achieves 0.65% attack success with 1.45% utility loss — strongest published result April 2026
230	- **ToolHijacker (NDSS 2026):** Tool-selection-phase attacks achieve 99.6% success vs StruQ — structured outputs alone insufficient if attacker influences tool selection
231	- See `docs/solutions/security-issues/prompt-injection-defense-layering-2026.md`

233	## LLM-as-Judge Style Bias Dominance (April 2026)
234	- **Style bias dominates: 0.76–0.92** across all 5 judge models (arxiv 2604.23178, 9 debiasing strategies); position bias now negligible (<0.04) due to improved instruction tuning
235	- **CoT gives the largest reduction: -0.14** — always prompt judge agents with step-by-step reasoning before verdict
236	- **Normalize markdown before aggregation:** Strip headers/bold/bullets from all reviewer outputs before passing to judge — formatting richness systematically inflates scores
237	- **Order-swap is sanity check, not primary debiasing** — retain it, but label it as secondary; style normalization + CoT + rubric-based criteria is current best practice
238	- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/llm-as-judge-style-bias-dominance.md`
239	
240	## Key File Locations
241	- Skill authoring: `plugins/yellow-core/skills/create-agent-skills/SKILL.md`
242	- Shared validation lib: `plugins/yellow-ruvector/hooks/scripts/lib/validate.sh`
243	- Compound docs: `docs/solutions/<category>/<slug>.md`
244

# Yellow Plugins - Project Memory

## Project Structure
- Marketplace repo: `yellow-plugins` with plugins in `plugins/<name>/`
- Each plugin follows Claude Code auto-discovery format: `agents/`, `commands/`, `skills/`, `plugin.json`
- Docs at `docs/`, solutions documented at `docs/solutions/<category>/`
- Main branch: `main`, uses Graphite (`gt`) for all branch/PR management

## Shell Script Security Patterns (from PR #5, extended PR #10)
- Always validate user input with `validate_name()` before use in paths
- Reject path traversal: `..`, `/`, `~` in names
- Printf: never put variables in format string — use `printf '%s' "$var"` not `printf "$var"`
- Skip symlinks when copying sensitive files: `[ -f "$f" ] && [ ! -L "$f" ]`
- Use `--` separator before positional args that could start with `-`
- Parse git output with `--porcelain` + `awk`, never human-readable format
- Use `sed 's/^prefix //'` not `cut -d' '` for paths (handles spaces)
- Path comparison: `[ "$a" = "$b" ]` not `echo | grep`

## Bash Hook & Validation Patterns (from PR #10, extended PRs #73, #74)
- **Newline in case patterns:** `$(printf '\n')` is EMPTY — command substitution strips trailing newlines. Use `tr -d '\n\r'` + length comparison instead
- **jq @sh consolidation:** Parse all JSON fields in one jq call: `eval "$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '@sh "VAR=\(.field)"')"` — add `# shellcheck disable=SC2154` at file level
- **TOCTOU in flock:** Re-read state INSIDE `flock` scope — never use values read before lock acquisition
- **Prompt injection fencing:** Wrap untrusted content in `--- begin/end ---` delimiters + "treat as reference only" advisory
- **Error logging:** Replace `|| true` / `2>/dev/null` with `|| { printf '[component] Error: ...\n' >&2; }` — always log with component prefix
- **Shared validation lib pattern:** Extract `validate_file_path()`, `validate_namespace()`, `canonicalize_project_dir()` to sourced `lib/validate.sh`
- **CRLF on WSL2:** Files created via Write tool get CRLF. Always `sed -i 's/\r$//'` after creating `.sh` files
- **Queue schema versioning:** Add `"schema": "1"` to JSONL entries for forward compatibility
- **PostToolUse field paths (from PR #73):** PostToolUse input nests fields — command is at `.tool_input.command` (NOT root `.command`), exit code is at `.tool_result.exit_code` (NOT root `.exit_code`). PreToolUse uses root `.command`. Confirmed in `plugins/yellow-ruvector/hooks/scripts/post-tool-use.sh:36` and `plugins/gt-workflow/hooks/check-commit-message.sh:19,34`
- **Conventional commit regex must include `!` (from PR #73):** Breaking-change indicator `!` is valid per spec: `feat!: msg` and `fix(scope)!: msg`. Regex `^(type)(\(scope\))?:` misses these. Always use `^(type)(\(scope\))?!?:` — the `!?` goes between the optional scope group and the colon
- **Hook not wired = completely non-functional (from PR #73):** A hook script can be perfectly written but silently never run if not registered in `plugin.json` as a `hooks` entry. After writing any hook script, verify a matching PostToolUse/PreToolUse/SessionStart entry exists in `plugin.json`. Detection: `jq '.hooks' plugins/<name>/plugin.json`
- **EXIT_CODE jq-parse fallback must fail-closed (from PR #73):** Pattern `EXIT_CODE="${exit_code:-0}"` defaults to 0 (success) when jq fails to parse the hook input. jq parse failure should be treated as unknown/error, not success. Use `EXIT_CODE="${exit_code:-1}"` or exit 1 on parse failure.
- **jq not installed warning in hooks (from PR #73):** Hook scripts that use jq must check `command -v jq` at the top and print a warning to stderr if missing — never silently fall through to undefined behavior.
- **SessionStart hooks must output `{"continue": true}` on all error paths (from PR #72):** If a SessionStart hook exits without outputting `{"continue": true}`, Claude Code blocks the entire session startup. Every error path — including jq failures, missing env vars, and API errors — must end with `printf '{"continue": true}\n'`. Pattern: `jq_command || { printf '[component] Error: jq failed\n' >&2; printf '{"continue": true}\n'; }`
- **`set -e` must NOT be used in hooks that output JSON (from this review session):** `set -euo pipefail` causes the script to exit on any non-zero command — before the required `{"continue": true}` line is printed. Use `set -uo pipefail` (drop `-e`) in ALL hook scripts that must output JSON. Add comment: `# Note: -e omitted intentionally — hook must output {"continue": true} on all paths`. Found in 3 hooks across 2 plugins in same review.
- **`json_exit()` helper pattern for hooks:** Define a helper at top of every hook script to centralize all exits: `json_exit() { local msg="${1:-}"; [ -n "$msg" ] && printf '[component] %s\n' "$msg" >&2; printf '{"continue": true}\n'; exit 0; }`. All early-exit paths call `json_exit` instead of bare `exit 0`. Guarantees no exit path forgets JSON output.
- **SessionStart must output JSON not plain text:** SessionStart hooks output `{"continue": true}` or `{"systemMessage": "...", "continue": true}`. Plain text output (e.g., `printf '[plugin] Warning: ...\n'` to stdout) is not a valid SessionStart response and causes unexpected behavior. System messages go in `systemMessage` JSON field; warnings go to stderr. See `docs/solutions/code-quality/hook-set-e-and-json-exit-pattern.md`
- **`CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` unset warning in SessionStart hooks (from PR #72):** When falling back to `$PWD`, always warn: `printf '[component] Warning: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset; falling back to PWD (%s)\n' "$PWD" >&2`. Silent fallback produces misleading behavior when Claude Code is launched from an unexpected directory.
- See `docs/solutions/security-issues/yellow-ruvector-plugin-multi-agent-code-review.md`
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/posttooluse-hook-input-schema-field-paths.md`

## Command File Anti-Patterns (from PR #35 review)
- **curl split:** Never `head -n -1` / `tail -1` — use `curl -o "$BODY_FILE" -w '%{http_code}'` (temp file pattern)
- **ToolSearch required:** Every command using deferred MCP tools must include `ToolSearch` in `allowed-tools`
- **subagent_type = agent name:** Must match `name:` field in agent frontmatter, NOT plugin directory name
- **subagent_type must be literal (from PR #71):** When prose says "Spawn X via Task", the exact `subagent_type: "plugin:dir:name"` string must be spelled out. LLMs guess wrong when it's left implicit — they use directory names or descriptive names instead of the frontmatter `name:` value
- **yq in $():** Exit code lost — parse frontmatter once, check exit, use `yq -r '@sh'` consolidation
- **yq -r required for raw string output (from PR #74):** kislyuk/yq returns quoted strings without `-r` flag: `yq '.status'` returns `"pending"` (with double-quotes), NOT `pending`. This causes ALL string comparisons (`case "$STATUS"`, `[ "$STATUS" = pending ]`) to silently fail — every transition matches `"pending"` not `pending`. Always use `yq -r '.field'` for string fields. This is distinct from the variant check below.
- **yq variant check (from PR #74):** `command -v yq` passes for both kislyuk/yq and mikefarah/yq — they are incompatible. Always add: `yq --help 2>&1 | grep -qi 'jq wrapper\|kislyuk' || { printf 'wrong yq variant\n' >&2; exit 1; }` when using `-y`/`@sh` flags
- **BASH_SOURCE[0]:** Not portable in Claude Code — use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/lib/validate.sh` instead
- **Dedup ordering:** Dedup check must run AFTER the data it keys on is collected (e.g., workflow name from failure-analyst)
- **M3 before bulk writes — no threshold (from PR #74):** AskUserQuestion showing count+titles is ALWAYS required before any loop that creates N issues/files. Never gate it on a count threshold (e.g., "if more than 20"). Applies for N=1 too.
- **AskUserQuestion for mid-step input (from PR #74):** Prose saying "ask as follow-up question in your response" does NOT pause execution. Every mid-step user input must use the `AskUserQuestion` tool. Every Cancel/No branch must have an explicit stop instruction.
- **AskUserQuestion "Other" is the ONLY free-text button (from PR #74):** Button labels like "Submit reason", "Enter date", or any custom label do NOT open a free-text input field. Only the literal button labeled "Other" opens free-text input in Claude Code's AskUserQuestion UI. When free-text input is needed (e.g., defer reason, custom message), use the "Other" label.
- **Agent step skip guards must be explicit prose (from PR #71):** When a step is conditional (e.g., "only if P1/P2 findings exist"), the agent will proceed unless the condition's false branch is stated as a literal instruction: "If [condition not met], skip this step." without that sentence, LLMs continue executing. Every optional step needs an explicit skip instruction as its first line.
- **$VAR in bash code blocks (from PR #74):** Each Bash tool call is a fresh subprocess — variables don't survive between calls. Any `$VAR` in a bash code block must be derived in that same block, or the LLM must be instructed to substitute the actual value inline. Never reference `$TODO_PATH` or similar in a code snippet that doesn't define it.
- **Heredoc for user-supplied free text (from PR #74):** When a command receives user-typed input (defer reason, message, description) and passes it to a shell function, use heredoc or `printf '%s'` — never inline substitution. User input containing `"`, backticks, or `$` produces malformed shell. Pattern: `REASON=$(cat <<'EOF'\n...\nEOF\n)` then pass as `"$REASON"`.
- **Heredoc delimiter collision with user input (P1):** Short, common English words as heredoc delimiters (`REASON`, `EOF`, `END`, `INPUT`, `MSG`) close prematurely if user types that word on its own line — silently truncates captured text and may execute trailing content as shell. Always use `__EOF_<CONTEXT>__` style delimiters (e.g., `<<'__EOF_DEFER_REASON__'`). Always quote the delimiter (`<<'...'` not `<<...`). See `docs/solutions/security-issues/heredoc-delimiter-collision.md`
- **Derived value validation:** Always validate $REPO, $ISSUE_ID, $SESSION_URL immediately after derivation
- **--flag missing value:** Guard with `[ -z "${2:-}" ] || printf '%s' "${2:-}" | grep -q '^--'`
- **Prerequisites as steps:** `command -v tool` + `gh auth status` as executable steps, not prose
- **Hook command scope must be exhaustive (from PR #73):** When writing a hook that validates or intercepts a commit message, enumerate ALL commands in the plugin that create commits — not just the most common ones. For gt-workflow: `gt modify`, `gt commit`, AND `gt create <name> -m "msg"` all produce commit messages. Missing even one command means the hook silently passes bad messages through that path.
- **Functions don't survive bash blocks (from PR #259):** Re-define every helper in each block that calls it; source a lib file for DRY. See `bash-block-subshell-isolation-in-command-files.md`
- **`has_userconfig` dead-code + credential echo (from PR #259):** Key absent from `plugin.json.userConfig` → call always returns false → dead branch. Never echo any portion of a credential value.
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/claude-code-command-authoring-anti-patterns.md`

## Plugin Authoring Quality Rules
- Agent `.md` files: 120 lines = audit threshold, not hard max. Accept up to 200 lines for multi-step workflows with novel algorithms; up to 300 lines if every section encodes content Claude cannot infer (lookup tables, scoring algorithms, security fencing, multi-branch UX). Split above 300 lines. Anthropic's SKILL.md hard guidance is 500 lines. See `docs/research/do-we-actually-need-a-120-line-maximum-o.md`
- Every agent/skill description must include "Use when..." trigger clause
- Command `allowed-tools` must list every tool used in the body
- Commands use `$ARGUMENTS` placeholder, never hardcoded values
- SKILL.md must have three standard headings: `## What It Does`, `## When to Use`, `## Usage`; subsections inside Usage are `###`
- All files must use LF line endings (configure `.gitattributes`)
- **Skill frontmatter attribute:** `user-invokable` (with k), NOT `user-invocable` (with c)
- **Skill and agent descriptions:** must be single-line — YAML folded scalars (`description: >`) AND multi-line single-quoted strings are NOT parsed by Claude Code's frontmatter parser. Both silently truncate the description. Applies to ALL `.md` plugin files (SKILL.md and agent `.md`). On-touch rule: whenever any agent/skill `.md` is modified, grep `'^description: [>|]'` AND check for single-quoted values that wrap to the next line. See `docs/solutions/code-quality/skill-frontmatter-attribute-and-format-requirements.md`
- **Install command format:** `/plugin marketplace add owner/repo` (short form works, no full HTTPS URL needed)
- **Stale dep name in CLAUDE.md after plugin switch (from PR #70):** When a plugin's dependency changes (e.g., `compound-engineering` → `yellow-core`), grep the plugin's CLAUDE.md "Known Limitations" and "Cross-Plugin Agent References" sections for the old plugin name — stale references cause agents to invoke a non-existent plugin. Detection: `grep -r '<old-plugin-name>' plugins/<name>/CLAUDE.md`

## Plugin Manifest Validation (from public release 2026-02-18, extended PR #66)
- **`repository` must be string:** Claude Code rejects npm-style `{"type":"git","url":"..."}` objects. Use plain URL string: `"repository": "https://github.com/owner/repo"`
- **`hooks` must be inline:** File path references (`"./hooks/hooks.json"`) rejected. Inline hook definitions as JSON object directly in plugin.json
- **hooks.json reference file:** If a `hooks/hooks.json` file exists alongside inline hooks in `plugin.json`, add a `_comment` key to it (`"_comment": "Reference only — Claude Code reads inline hooks from plugin.json"`) so future maintainers do not treat the file as authoritative. Also keep all numeric fields (e.g., `timeout`) in sync — `hooks/hooks.json` timeout drifting from `plugin.json` hooks timeout causes subtle behavior mismatches (from PR #74)
- **No unknown keys:** `marketplace.json` entries reject any key not in schema (e.g., `"id"`). Use `"name"` as identifier. The `"changelog"` key is NOT recognized by the remote validator — do not add it to plugin.json
- **Local CI ≠ remote validation:** `pnpm validate:schemas` passing does NOT guarantee Claude Code's validator accepts it. Always test install on a fresh machine before release
- **Schema alignment:** Add `"additionalProperties": false` to local schemas to catch unknown keys in CI
- **JSON trailing comma on deletion:** When removing the last property from a JSON `"properties"` block, the preceding property retains a trailing comma — invalid JSON. Always run `jq empty` after any property removal
- See `docs/solutions/build-errors/claude-code-plugin-manifest-validation-errors.md`
- See `docs/solutions/build-errors/plugin-json-changelog-key-schema-drift-remote-validator.md`

## Plugin Review Consistency Patterns (from PR #8)
- Security pattern codes (M3, C1, H1) must name the concrete tool: "confirm via AskUserQuestion" not just "M3 confirmation"
- Shared validation rules: copy ALL steps from skill — never omit any (e.g., "strip HTML")
- Error handling refs: enumerate categories, don't just say "see skill for errors"
- Brainstorm/plan numeric claims: verify command count, line budget, agent count match code before PR
- AI reviewer false positives: run `pnpm validate:plugins` + check precedent in other plugins before accepting
- Top-level `mcpServers` in plugin.json is valid (yellow-linear precedent, passes validation)

## GitHub GraphQL Shell Patterns (from PR #9)
- jq pipelines: always capture exit code — `OUTPUT=$(...| jq '...') || { error; exit 1; }`
- JSON construction: use `jq -n --arg` not `printf '{"key":"%s"}'`
- Nullable GitHub fields: use `// "ghost"` or `// "default"` fallback
- GraphQL `first:N`: always include `pageInfo { hasNextPage }` + warn on truncation
- Error pattern matching: use complete phrases (`*"already resolved"*`) not single words
- ShellCheck SC2016: disable on **separate line** above GraphQL queries — inline comments cause SC1073
- Cross-plugin agent refs: distinguish plugin `.md` files from Task `subagent_type` registry entries
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/github-graphql-shell-script-patterns.md`

## Agent Workflow Security Patterns (from PR #9)
- Human-in-the-loop: commands that push LLM-generated code must `AskUserQuestion` before `gt submit`
- Prompt injection boundaries: agents processing untrusted input (PR comments, issues) need explicit safety rules
- Path traversal in derived paths: validate category/slug contain only `[a-z0-9-]` before constructing file paths. Use `^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (not `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$`) to also reject trailing and consecutive hyphens
- Custom-rule bot noise: CodeAnt `[custom_rule]` comments about @Devin tagging — dismiss for agent/config files
- See `docs/solutions/security-issues/agent-workflow-security-patterns.md`

## Shell Documentation Patterns (from PR #7)
- Quote ALL variables in code examples: `"$var"` in tests and case statements
- Add `command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1` checks before any tool usage
- Error messages must distinguish state categories (not-yet-running vs completed)
- TOCTOU: re-fetch state after user interaction, before destructive action
- HTTP 429: must exit or retry — never fall through to next code block
- Token format: define in one place (shared skill), grep for stale prefixes
- Numeric constraints: cross-check polling limits vs time-box guidelines
- plugin.json `commands`: must include ALL tools used by agents/commands
- POST curl examples: always include `Content-Type: application/json` header

## Parallel Todo Resolution Patterns (from PR #37, 2026-02-22)
- **File-based grouping:** When resolving N todos in parallel, spawn one agent per FILE (not per todo). Todos that share a file must go to the same agent — last writer wins in concurrent edits
- **Dependency mapping:** Build todo→file map first, invert to file→todos, then spawn. Conflict sets = todos sharing any file (transitive closure)
- **Cascade closures:** YAGNI removal todos (e.g., 058 removes a step) close sibling todos targeting that step (e.g., 049). Identify before spawning — assign to closing agent with "close without changes"
- **Within-file ordering:** Sequential deps handled naturally by single agent: structural changes → content changes; removals → additions; guards → logic that uses them
- **Cross-file todo renaming:** If todo 046 affects 7 files, designate ONE agent as canonical closer for that todo file rename. All others: "Do not rename T-046"
- **gt modify multi-line commit:** Use separate `-m` flags: `gt modify -c -m "subject" -m "body"`. Heredoc in `$()` substitution fails (treated as single unknown argument)
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/parallel-todo-resolution-file-based-grouping.md`

## Parallel Agent Orchestration Patterns (from PR #11)
- **File-ownership grouping:** Before launching parallel fix agents, map each todo to its target files. Group non-conflicting todos; run groups sequentially, agents within groups in parallel
- **Defer structural refactors:** If a todo (e.g., DRY refactor) overlaps with many targeted fixes, defer it to a separate PR
- **Agent type names:** Use exact Task tool registry names (`pr-review-toolkit:X`), NOT inferred plugin paths (`compound-engineering:review:X`)
- **Batch triage:** For 10+ findings, rename todo files `pending→ready` in bulk rather than one-by-one approval
- **Bulk operation safety:** Explicitly exclude deferred items from batch status updates; verify after
- **Skill trimming:** Cut LLM training data duplication, keep safety rules + trigger clauses + validation patterns
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/parallel-multi-agent-review-orchestration.md`

## Multi-Agent Review Patterns (from PR #18)
- Stop after 2 rounds unless P1s remain; expect ~38% FP rate in re-reviews; test empirically before creating todos
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/multi-agent-re-review-false-positive-patterns.md`

## PR Merge Unblocking Patterns (from session 2026-02-17)
- **mergeStateStatus**: DIRTY=conflicts, UNSTABLE=CI failing not required (mergeable), BLOCKED=required checks
- **CRLF blocks merge**: `git status --short | awk '{print $2}' | xargs -I{} sed -i 's/\r$//' {} && git add -u && git commit -m "chore: normalize CRLF"`
- **Stash list is global**: `git stash drop` affects ALL worktrees; never `git checkout branch -- .` (copies entire tree)
- See `docs/solutions/workflow/wsl2-crlf-pr-merge-unblocking.md`

## Prettier & Documentation Patterns (from PR #24)
- **Metadata block:** `proseWrap: always` merges `**key**: value` lines into paragraphs. Fix: add `<!-- prettier-ignore -->`
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/public-release-stale-references-and-prettier-formatting.md`

## ruvector CLI & MCP Patterns (from session 2026-02-18)
- **CLI:** `ruvector hooks init`, `ruvector mcp start`; no `vector_db_*` tools (fictitious LLM training data)
- **hooks_recall schema:** `query` + `top_k`; returns `score` (NOT `similarity`); no `namespace` param
- See `docs/solutions/integration-issues/ruvector-cli-and-mcp-tool-name-mismatches.md`

## CI Schema Drift Patterns (from PR #31)
- **Two-validator problem:** local CI schema and Claude Code remote validator can diverge — check both
- See `docs/solutions/build-errors/ci-schema-drift-hooks-inline-vs-string.md`

## MCP Bundled Server Tool Naming (from PR #33)
- **Prefix formula:** `mcp__plugin_{pluginName}_{serverName}__{toolName}`
- **allowed-tools drift on rename:** after any tool rename, grep for old name across ALL delegating commands
- See `docs/solutions/integration-issues/mcp-bundled-server-tool-naming-and-plugin-authoring-patterns.md`

## Parallel Todo/Agent Orchestration Patterns (from PRs #37, #11)
- Spawn one agent per FILE (not per todo); todos sharing a file must go to same agent — last writer wins
- Map todos to target files before launching; run conflicting groups sequentially, non-conflicting in parallel
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/parallel-todo-resolution-file-based-grouping.md`

## pnpm Strict Isolation & Node.js Root Script Deps (from PR #47)
- Root scripts need deps in root `package.json` devDependencies; stage both `package.json` AND lockfile after install
- See `docs/solutions/build-errors/pnpm-strict-isolation-and-regex-escape-pitfalls.md`

## Multi-Phase Orchestrator & Agent Migration Patterns
- **exit 1 does NOT stop LLM:** Add "If the above exits non-zero, stop." prose after every error path
- **GIT_ROOT before all paths:** `GIT_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)`. Slug regex: `^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$`
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/agent-migration-audit-patterns.md`

## Structured Filename Glob Counting Bugs (from PR #72)
- Use anchored bash regex for structured filenames; glob fast-path can double-count when slug overlaps state field
- See `docs/solutions/logic-errors/structured-filename-glob-counting-bugs.md`

## Cross-Plugin Documentation Correctness (from PRs #75, #76)
- Read `name:` from command frontmatter; read plugin CLAUDE.md "Required Environment Variables" for credentials
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/cross-plugin-documentation-correctness.md`

## Plugin Agent Authoring Anti-Patterns (from PR #265, 2026-04-26, extended round-2)
- **Multi-file MCP count drift:** Every MCP add/remove must update counts in README (2+ locations), plugin `package.json`, and setup probe lists atomically — run `rg '[0-9]+ MCP' README.md` before opening PR; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/stale-env-var-docs-and-prose-count-drift.md`
- **Subjective fallback thresholds:** "No useful results / score thin / sparse" gives LLM interpretive latitude to skip fallback entirely — replace with `totalResults < N` numeric guards, document threshold rationale inline; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/agent-prose-fallback-threshold-anti-pattern.md`
- **Classification ↔ probe coupling:** Every ToolSearch visibility criterion in `setup/all.md` Step 2 must have a matching probe entry in Step 1.5 (keyword list + resolved-name list + count); adding a criterion without the probe causes silent misfire on every run; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/setup-classification-probe-coupling.md`
- **Duplicate MCP URL = N OAuth pop-ups (from PR #265 round-2):** Same MCP server URL registered in multiple `plugin.json` files = separate OAuth client per Claude Code plugin namespace = N browser OAuth prompts on first use. Register each MCP server in exactly one plugin; consuming plugins detect via ToolSearch + document as optional cross-plugin dep. Pre-PR check: `rg '"url".*mcp\.' plugins/*/plugin.json`; see `docs/solutions/integration-issues/duplicate-mcp-url-double-oauth.md`
- **Agent prose `result.X < N` field-rename brittleness (from PR #265 round-2):** LLM evaluates missing field as 0, silently activating fallback on every call when MCP server renames field. Fix: document fail-closed inline ("if `result.totalResults` missing, treat as 0 and log warning") AND add integration test asserting field is present and numeric on sparse-results queries; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/agent-prose-field-path-brittleness.md`
- **Real-prefix test fixture strings trigger secret scanners (from PR #265 round-2):** `cer_sk_invalid_key_for_test_only_xxx` matches TruffleHog/Gitleaks prefix rules — CI noise or allowlist blind spot. Use vendor-neutral prefixes like `invalid-test-key-xxxx` or `test_fixture_not_a_real_credential_00`; never add file-level allowlist entries; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/test-fixture-secret-scanner-false-positive.md`

## JSON Schema & Validator Correctness Patterns (from PR #257, 2026-04-28)
- **`typeof === 'object'` bypassed by array form:** When a field gains an array-form variant, all existing `typeof value === 'object'` guards silently match arrays too — add `&& !Array.isArray(value)` everywhere; see `docs/solutions/logic-errors/json-schema-typeof-array-bypass.md`
- **Unconstrained `{ "type": "object" }` in oneOf = accept-all:** Pair every naked object branch with `minProperties: 1` or `required`; audit validator script consumers atomically when adding new schema discriminants; see `docs/solutions/logic-errors/json-schema-typeof-array-bypass.md`

## Cross-Plugin Frontmatter Sweeps & Canonical Skill Drift (from PR #257, 2026-04-28)
- **Sweep boundary = grep, not mental model:** Derive sweep file list via `rg` before applying — author's concept of "review agents" silently excludes equivalent agents in other plugins; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/frontmatter-sweep-and-canonical-skill-drift.md`
- **Canonical skill blocks must be verbatim copy:** Paraphrased copies (even one dropped sentence) re-introduce the drift SKILL.md was meant to eliminate; diff against source before committing; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/frontmatter-sweep-and-canonical-skill-drift.md`

## Shell Helper & Plugin Install Patterns (from PR #248, 2026-04-28)
- **`printf '%b'` interprets `\033`/`\n` from caller args:** Colored `error()`/`warning()` helpers using `'%bError: %s%b\n'` let any caller-passed external output inject terminal escapes. Use `'\033[0;31mError: %s\033[0m\n' "$1"` — literal ANSI in format string + `%s` for content; see `docs/solutions/security-issues/printf-percent-b-terminal-escape-injection.md`
- **Smoke-test the MCP entrypoint subcommand at install time:** Plugin install scripts that verify `<tool> --version` still leave a silent 0-tools failure mode if `plugin.json` invokes a subcommand (e.g., `mempalace mcp`) absent from the installed version. Add `<tool> <subcommand> --help >/dev/null 2>&1 || warning "..."` after PATH+version checks; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/plugin-install-mcp-subcommand-smoke-test.md`
- **`have_X` + `run_X` helper pair beats token-array protocol:** When a tool has multiple invocations (`python3 -m pip` vs `pip3` vs `pip`) called from 2+ sites, a `mapfile -t cmd < <(detect_X)` protocol propagates ceremony to every caller. Use `have_X()` predicate (sets `X_CMD_USED` for messages) + `run_X()` dispatcher with a `case` — call sites become plain function calls; see `docs/solutions/code-quality/shell-tool-detection-helper-pair-pattern.md`

## Workflow Conventions
- Use `gt modify -c` (not deprecated `gt commit create`) for commits; `gt submit --no-interactive` to push
- **`gt commit create` branch-switching bug:** After `gt stack submit`, use `git commit -m` directly; `gt modify -c -m` is safe

## Upstream Concept-Fork Snapshotting Protocol (from session 2026-04-28)
- **Lock SHA on authoring:** Every "Phase 0: fetch upstream" step must record the locked tag/SHA + snapshot date — not just "fetch from main"
- **Audit release notes before each Wave:** Run `gh api repos/ORG/REPO/releases` for changes since last plan revision; look for renames, deprecated aliases, artifact path moves, rubric replacements
- **Version-pin to latest patch with known-issue fixes:** Multi-agent pipeline PRs must record `minimum-upstream-version: vX.Y.Z` citing the fix commit for any queuing/silent-failure bugs — do not snapshot main without checking issue tracker
- **CE April 2026 concrete example:** v3.0.0 (2026-04-22) broke all skill names to `ce-` prefix; v3.2.0 replaced LFG rubric + moved artifacts `.context/` → `/tmp`; v3.3.1 fixed reviewer-queuing silent-drop (#716)
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/upstream-concept-fork-snapshot-protocol.md`

## Prompt Injection Defense Layering (April 2026)
- **Model-level fencing degrades:** Sandwich defense goes from 0.4% → 3.8% leak rate under 277-round sustained attack (arxiv 2604.23887) — not a primary control
- **App-layer output filtering = only zero-leak defense:** Filter agent outputs for injection markers before any sensitive sink (Write tool, Bash, git op, API mutation)
- **ROLP is the highest-leverage control:** Untrusted content (PR diffs, issue bodies, API payloads) must NEVER appear in system prompt — inject only into human turn / tool result positions
- **AgentVisor STI protocol** (arxiv 2604.24118): Suitability/Taint/Integrity labeling achieves 0.65% attack success with 1.45% utility loss — strongest published result April 2026
- **ToolHijacker (NDSS 2026):** Tool-selection-phase attacks achieve 99.6% success vs StruQ — structured outputs alone insufficient if attacker influences tool selection
- See `docs/solutions/security-issues/prompt-injection-defense-layering-2026.md`

## LLM-as-Judge Style Bias Dominance (April 2026)
- **Style bias dominates: 0.76–0.92** across all 5 judge models (arxiv 2604.23178, 9 debiasing strategies); position bias now negligible (<0.04) due to improved instruction tuning
- **CoT gives the largest reduction: -0.14** — always prompt judge agents with step-by-step reasoning before verdict
- **Normalize markdown before aggregation:** Strip headers/bold/bullets from all reviewer outputs before passing to judge — formatting richness systematically inflates scores
- **Order-swap is sanity check, not primary debiasing** — retain it, but label it as secondary; style normalization + CoT + rubric-based criteria is current best practice
- See `docs/solutions/code-quality/llm-as-judge-style-bias-dominance.md`

## Key File Locations
- Skill authoring: `plugins/yellow-core/skills/create-agent-skills/SKILL.md`
- Shared validation lib: `plugins/yellow-ruvector/hooks/scripts/lib/validate.sh`
- Compound docs: `docs/solutions/<category>/<slug>.md`

# Feature: EveryInc/Compound-Engineering Selective Merge — Wave 3 (Parallel)

**Date:** 2026-04-28 (decomposition split 2026-04-29)
**Source plan:** `plans/everyinc-merge.md` (backbone — must merge to `main` before this plan runs)
**Status:** Deferred. Run after the backbone (PRs #1–#7 in the source plan) merges to `main`.

---

## Overview

This plan decomposes Wave 3 of the EveryInc merge into **12 parallel feature branches**, each rooted at `main` after the Wave 2 keystone (`plans/everyinc-merge.md` PR #7) has merged. Branches are independent of each other (different files, no cross-dependencies) and can be developed and reviewed in parallel.

All implementation task definitions (W3.1, W3.2, W3.3, W3.4, W3.5, W3.6, W3.7, W3.8, W3.10, W3.11, W3.12, W3.13b, W3.14, W3.15) live in `plans/everyinc-merge.md` under the "Wave 3" section. This file only specifies the parallel stack decomposition; do not duplicate task content here.

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 0: Pre-Wave Preparation (Wave 3)

Before starting the parallel stack:

- [ ] 0.1 Re-fetch the latest `EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin` `main` SHA. If it has advanced beyond the backbone's locked SHA (`e5b397c9d1883354f03e338dd00f98be3da39f9f` / `compound-engineering-v3.3.2`), check `gh api repos/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin/releases?per_page=10` for new releases and review their changes for impact on Wave 3 tasks. Lock a new SHA for this wave.
- [ ] 0.2 Fetch upstream snapshots for Wave 3 tasks not already snapshotted in the backbone. Required additions (committed in PR #1 below):
  - `skills/ce-debug/` (W3.1)
  - `skills/ce-doc-review/SKILL.md` + agents `ce-coherence-reviewer`, `ce-design-lens-reviewer`, `ce-feasibility-reviewer`, `ce-product-lens-reviewer`, `ce-scope-guardian-reviewer`, `ce-adversarial-document-reviewer` (W3.2)
  - Agents `ce-cli-readiness-reviewer`, `ce-cli-agent-readiness-reviewer`, `ce-agent-native-reviewer`; skills `ce-agent-native-architecture/`, `ce-agent-native-audit/` (W3.5)
  - `skills/ce-compound-refresh/` (W3.10)
  - `skills/ce-ideate/` (W3.11)
  - `agents/ce-session-historian.agent.md` (W3.12)
  - `skills/ce-optimize/` incl. `schema.yaml` and `README.md` (W3.14)
  - `agents/ce-api-contract-reviewer.agent.md` (W3.15 — adapted to plugin-contract focus)
  - `skills/ce-worktree/` (W3.4 reference)
- [ ] 0.3 Validate snapshot bodies are reasonable; flag any > 500 lines for extract-only treatment (per user feedback 2026-04-29: file size is content-driven, not capped — 500 is a soft outer bound).
- [ ] 0.4 Run `pnpm validate:schemas && pnpm test:unit` baseline on `main` after backbone merges. Record green baseline.
- [ ] 0.5 Read the source plan's "Wave 3" section in full so the implementation context is loaded.

For full task content, see `plans/everyinc-merge.md` "Wave 3: P1 Adoptions (reviewed by Wave 2 pipeline)" section.

## Acceptance Criteria

Per-component acceptance is enumerated inside each task in the source plan. The Wave 3 effort is "done" when all 12 parallel PRs below merge to `main`. Each PR is reviewed by the Wave 2 pipeline (the keystone shipped in backbone PR #7). No Wave 3 PR introduces new `Bash` in any reviewer agent (Wave 1 rule applies, enforced by `scripts/validate-agent-authoring.js` Rule X added in backbone PR #5).

## Stack Decomposition

<!-- stack-topology: parallel -->
<!-- stack-trunk: main -->

12 parallel branches from `main`. Each is independent (no cross-branch file overlap) and can be developed, reviewed, and merged in any order. Branch creation is just-in-time per `/workflows:work` Phase 1b parallel topology.

### 1. feat/ce-debug-skill
- **Type:** feat
- **Description:** ce-debug analog skill — test-first systematic debugging, causal chain tracing
- **Scope:** NEW plugins/yellow-core/skills/debugging/SKILL.md
- **Tasks:** W3.1
- **Depends on:** (backbone merged)

### 2. feat/yellow-docs-doc-review
- **Type:** feat
- **Description:** ce-doc-review in yellow-docs — 6 personas + adversarial-document-reviewer + new /docs:review command
- **Scope:** NEW plugins/yellow-docs/agents/review/{coherence,design-lens,feasibility,product-lens,scope-guardian,security-lens,adversarial-document}-reviewer.md, NEW plugins/yellow-docs/commands/docs/review.md
- **Tasks:** W3.2
- **Depends on:** (backbone merged)

### 3. feat/resolve-pr-cluster-and-actionability
- **Type:** feat
- **Description:** resolve-pr cross-invocation cluster analysis + actionability filter (CE PRs #480, #461)
- **Scope:** plugins/yellow-review/commands/review/resolve-pr.md
- **Tasks:** W3.3
- **Depends on:** (backbone merged)

### 4. fix/git-worktree-and-local-config-expansion
- **Type:** fix
- **Description:** git-worktree mise/direnv auto-trust + .git is-a-file detection; yellow-plugins.local.md schema expansion (full keys)
- **Scope:** plugins/yellow-core/skills/git-worktree/SKILL.md, plugins/yellow-core/skills/local-config/SKILL.md (extends from backbone PR #7)
- **Tasks:** W3.4, W3.6
- **Depends on:** (backbone merged)

### 5. feat/agent-native-reviewers
- **Type:** feat
- **Description:** cli-readiness, agent-cli-readiness, agent-native reviewers + agent-native-architecture/audit skills
- **Scope:** NEW plugins/yellow-review/agents/review/{cli-readiness,agent-cli-readiness,agent-native}-reviewer.md, NEW plugins/plugin-dev/skills/{agent-native-architecture,agent-native-audit}/SKILL.md (or under plugins/yellow-core/skills/ if plugin-dev creation is out of scope)

### 6. feat/compound-lifecycle-skill

- **Scope:** NEW plugins/yellow-core/skills/compound-lifecycle/SKILL.md, NEW docs/solutions/archived/

### 7. feat/yellow-debt-confidence-calibration

### 8. feat/ideation-skill

### 9. feat/cross-vendor-session-history

- **Description:** cross-vendor session-historian (Claude Code + Devin + Codex backends) with hybrid query (BM25 + cosine + RRF) + secret redaction
- **Scope:** NEW plugins/yellow-core/skills/session-history/SKILL.md, NEW plugins/yellow-core/agents/workflow/session-historian.md

### 10. feat/optimize-skill
- **Type:** feat
- **Description:** ce-optimize analog — LLM-as-judge with parallel experiments, schema.yaml, two-run order-swap, judge_telemetry schema
- **Scope:** NEW plugins/yellow-core/skills/optimize/SKILL.md, NEW plugins/yellow-core/skills/optimize/schema.yaml
- **Tasks:** W3.14
- **Depends on:** (backbone merged)

### 11. docs/yellow-codex-and-composio-research

## Overview

This plan decomposes Wave 3 of the EveryInc merge into **12 parallel feature branches**, each rooted at `main` after the Wave 2 keystone (`plans/everyinc-merge.md` PR #7) has merged. Branches are independent of each other (different files, no cross-dependencies) and can be developed and reviewed in parallel.

All implementation task definitions (W3.1, W3.2, W3.3, W3.4, W3.5, W3.6, W3.7, W3.8, W3.10, W3.11, W3.12, W3.13b, W3.14, W3.15) live in `plans/everyinc-merge.md` under the "Wave 3" section. This file only specifies the parallel stack decomposition; do not duplicate task content here.

Lock a new SHA for this wave.

content.subject: Pre-execution: split plan + capture context docs
content.description: (a) Edit plans/everyinc-merge.md: change `<!-- stack-topology: mixed -->` to `linear`, trim Stack Decomposition to items #1-#6, update intro paragraph to describe backbone-only scope. (b) Create plans/everyinc-merge-wave3.md with `<!-- stack-topology: parallel -->` `<!-- stack-trunk: main -->` containing items #7-#18 renumbered #1-#12 with refs back to main plan for task details. (c) Decide where to commit the untracked context docs (plans, brainstorms, audit, new solutions). Recommendation: dedicated docs/ branch as item #0 of the stack so it lands on main before any implementation work.
content.activeForm: Splitting plan and staging context docs

content.subject: Phase 0: baseline green check
content.description: Run `pnpm install` if node_modules missing, then `pnpm validate:schemas && pnpm test:unit && pnpm test:integration`. Record green baseline. Per plan task 0.4. Required before any Wave 1 edits.

content.subject: Phase 0: lock CE upstream SHA + fetch Wave 1 snapshots
content.description: Per plan tasks 0.1-0.3: (1) Fetch latest main SHA of EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin via gh api. Verify >= v3.3.1 (2026-04-28). Record SHA. (2) For Wave 1 #3 and #4, fetch upstream file bodies for: best-practices-researcher, repo-research-analyst, git-history-analyzer, spec-flow-analyzer, performance-oracle/reviewer split (CE), security-sentinel/reviewer/lens split (CE), pr-comment-resolver (CE PR #490). Save to RESEARCH/upstream-snapshots/<sha>/<path>. (3) Validate each snapshot file <= 500 lines.
content.activeForm: Locking SHA and fetching Wave 1 upstream snapshots

content.subject: Backbone #1: docs/everyinc-merge-plan
content.description: New backbone item #0. Branch: docs/everyinc-merge-plan. Commits the untracked context files: plans/everyinc-merge.md, plans/everyinc-merge-wave3.md, docs/brainstorms/2026-04-28-everyinc-*.md (3 files), docs/research/merge-plan-completeness-audit-april-2026.md, RESEARCH/upstream-snapshots/<sha>/* (Phase 0 snapshots), and the 3 new docs/solutions/ entries (llm-as-judge-style-bias-dominance, upstream-concept-fork-snapshot-protocol, prompt-injection-defense-layering-2026). Excludes .entire/. After commit: gt submit, ask user via AskUserQuestion before submit per yellow-plugins convention.

content.subject: Backbone #2: chore/remove-context7-mcp (W1.1)
content.description: Remove bundled context7 MCP entry and clean 8+ reference files. Files: yellow-core/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (remove mcpServers block), yellow-core/agents/research/best-practices-researcher.md (drop context7 tools), yellow-core/commands/statusline/setup.md (remove context7 references), yellow-core/CLAUDE.md and README.md (remove context7 docs), yellow-research/agents/research/code-researcher.md (remove tools+routing), yellow-research/commands/research/code.md and setup.md (remove allowed-tools entries), yellow-research/CLAUDE.md (remove optional-deps entry). Verify code-researcher still routes coherently. Changeset: yellow-core patch, yellow-research patch.

content.subject: Backbone #3: chore/strip-bash-from-reviewers (W1.2)
content.description: Strip Bash from 14 reviewer agents. Files (yellow-core/agents/review/): architecture-strategist, code-simplicity-reviewer, pattern-recognition-specialist, performance-oracle, polyglot-reviewer, security-sentinel, test-coverage-analyst. (yellow-review/agents/review/): code-reviewer, code-simplifier, comment-analyzer, pr-test-analyzer, silent-failure-hunter, type-design-analyzer. (yellow-codex/agents/review/): codex-reviewer. Keep ToolSearch + ast-grep MCP tools where present (read-only). Changeset: yellow-core minor, yellow-review minor, yellow-codex patch.

content.subject: Backbone #4: refactor/repair-drifted-agents (W1.3)
content.description: Repair 6 drifted agents per upstream-snapshot parity. Files (yellow-core/agents/research/): best-practices-researcher (skills-first phase-1 parity), repo-research-analyst (CE PR #327 structured tech scan), git-history-analyzer (frontmatter parity). Files (yellow-core/agents/workflow/): spec-flow-analyzer (frontmatter parity). Splits in yellow-core/agents/review/: performance-oracle → keep oracle (analyzer) + create performance-reviewer.md. security-sentinel → keep sentinel + create security-reviewer.md + security-lens.md. Cross-reference any subagent_type:"yellow-core:security-sentinel" callers and update if signature changes. REQUIRES Phase 0 snapshots.

content.subject: Backbone #5: fix/pr-comment-fence-verify-and-validation (W1.4 + W1.5)
content.description: W1.4: Verify pr-comment-resolver.md (yellow-review/agents/workflow) fences match CE PR #490; bring missing protections to parity (deepen-plan note: largely no-op, fences already exist). Update resolve-pr.md Step 5 spawn block. Add fencing note to pr-review-workflow/SKILL.md. W1.5: Add Rule X to scripts/validate-agent-authoring.js: any agent at agents/review/*.md must NOT have Bash/Write/Edit in tools:. Add fixture test in tests/integration/. REQUIRES Phase 0 CE PR #490 snapshot.

content.subject: Backbone #6: feat/knowledge-compounder-track-schema (W2.0a)
content.description: Update yellow-core/agents/workflow/knowledge-compounder.md with new frontmatter fields: track (bug|knowledge), tags[], problem (one-line). Add context budget precheck (>200 lines → AskUserQuestion split). Backfill 51 docs/solutions/ entries via new scripts/backfill-solution-frontmatter.js. Heuristic: logic-errors/security-issues/build-errors → bug; code-quality/workflow/integration-issues → knowledge. Flag security-issues/* containing "audit"/"threat model"/"pre-implementation" for manual review. Make script idempotent + commit it (re-runnable). REQUIRES Phase 0 ce-compound snapshot.

content.subject: Backbone #7: feat/review-pr-keystone-rewrite (W2.1-W2.9)
content.description: Keystone PR. NEW: yellow-core/agents/research/learnings-researcher.md (BM25-via-ruvector primary path, glob fallback). NEW: 5 persona reviewers under yellow-review/agents/review/ (correctness, maintainability, reliability, project-standards, adversarial). RENAME: yellow-review/agents/review/code-reviewer.md → project-compliance-reviewer.md (with deprecation stub at old path). REWRITE: yellow-review/commands/review/review-pr.md (preserve existing 13-step structure incl. 3b/3c/9b; insert 3a base-fetch, 3d learnings-researcher, replace Step 4 with tiered persona dispatch + graceful-degradation guard, enforce compact-return Step 5, confidence-rubric Step 6). UPDATE: yellow-review/commands/review/review-all.md lines 75-96 to mirror. NEW: yellow-core/skills/local-config/SKILL.md (yellow-plugins.local.md schema). NEW: docs/solutions/code-quality/learnings-researcher-pre-pass-pattern.md. Update SKILL.md prose refs and all subagent_type references. Changeset: yellow-core minor, yellow-review MAJOR. REQUIRES Phase 0 snapshots: ce-learnings-researcher, ce-code-review SKILL.md, 5 persona agents.

content.subject: Wave 3 deferred (12 parallel branches)
content.description: DEFERRED until backbone (#0-#6) merges to main. Then re-invoke /workflows:work plans/everyinc-merge-wave3.md to run as parallel topology from main. Items: W3.1 ce-debug skill, W3.2 yellow-docs doc-review (6 personas + adversarial-document + new command), W3.3 resolve-pr cluster+actionability, W3.4+W3.6 git-worktree+local-config-expansion, W3.5 agent-native reviewers (3 reviewers + 2 skills; may require plugin-dev plugin creation), W3.10 compound-lifecycle skill, W3.11 ideation skill (Toulmin warrant + MIDAS three-phase), W3.12 cross-vendor session-history (Claude/Devin/Codex backends), W3.13b yellow-debt scanner schema v2.0 (breaking), W3.14 optimize skill (LLM-as-judge), W3.15 plugin-contract-reviewer, W3.7+W3.8 yellow-codex/composio research reports.
content.activeForm: Holding Wave 3 for parallel run after backbone merges

content.text:
Found the answer in `RESEARCH/MERGE_PLAN.md:77` and `every-plugin-research.md:375`. Important nuance: **we're not removing context7 itself — we're removing the *bundled* entry in `yellow-core/plugin.json`'s `mcpServers` block.**

The full reasoning:

1. **Upstream EveryInc removed bundled context7 in CE v2.62.0 (#486, 2026-04-03)** because *"users were ending up with two copies."* When yellow-core ships context7 as a bundled plugin MCP and the user already has context7 configured at their user level (which is the normal way to install MCPs), Claude Code registers two instances. That triggers the "Duplicate MCP URL = N OAuth pop-ups" pattern from your CLAUDE.md memory — separate OAuth client per plugin namespace = N browser prompts on first use.

2. **Context7 stays available — the install path moves.** Users who want context7 add it once at user level (~30 seconds, standard `/plugin` flow). They get exactly the same context7 they had before, just with one OAuth round-trip instead of two and no dual-namespace tool name collisions. yellow-core stops claiming a tool it can't fully control the lifecycle of.

3. **Code-side change is mechanical.** The 9 files reference tools named `mcp__plugin_yellow-core_context7__resolve-library-id` and `mcp__plugin_yellow-core_context7__query-docs`. Those exact tool names only exist when context7 is bundled inside yellow-core. Once unbundled, those tool names disappear; the user-level context7 has different (un-namespaced) tool names. So the references need updating either way — the question is whether yellow-research's `code-researcher` should fall back to user-level context7 (with the new tool names) or just drop it and route to Exa/Perplexity.

4. **Plan currently chooses "drop context7 tool reference."** yellow-research already routes to Exa, Perplexity, Tavily, ast-grep, and Ceramic. Library-doc lookup is well-covered by Exa+Perplexity (you set Ceramic.ai as default research backend in commit `635f58d`). The marginal value of keeping a context7 reference vs. dropping it is small.

**If you want to keep context7 referenced** (just unbundled), I can adjust W1.1 to: drop the `mcpServers` block from `yellow-core/plugin.json` and remove `mcp__plugin_yellow-core_context7__*` tool entries (those become invalid), but keep prose-level references like *"context7 (install separately if desired)"* in README/CLAUDE.md so users know it's a recommended user-level MCP.

How would you like to proceed?

Smallest surface area; matches CE upstream pattern; aligns with your Ceramic-as-default decision.
